title: -Contract Awards:  Du-All Safety LLC, Safe Business Solutions, and Safety Center, Inc. for Safety Training and Consulting Services to support the Public Works Department

sections.recommendation:
Adopt three Resolutions authorizing the City Manager to:
1. Execute a Contract and subsequent amendments with Du-All Safety, LLC dba
Du-All, for safety training and consulting services in an amount not to exceed
$70,000 for the initial contract period March 1, 2026, through February 28, 2029,
with the option to extend the contract term for two additional years, for a total
contract term not to exceed five years.
2. Execute a contract and subsequent amendments with Safe Business Solutions
dba SBS, for safety training and consulting in an amount not to exceed $70,000
for the initial contract period March 1, 2026, through February 28, 2029, with the
option to extend the contract term for two additional years, for a total contract
term not to exceed five years.
3. Execute a Contract and subsequent amendments with Safety Center for safety
training and consulting in an amount not to exceed $70,000 for the initial
contract period March 1, 2026, through February 28, 2029, with the option to
extend the contract term for two additional years, for a total contract term not to
exceed five years.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
The funding necessary to support these contracts is available in the FY 2026 Public
Works Zero Waste Fund (Fund 601), Sanitary Sewer Fund (Fund 611), Clean Storm
Fund (Fund 616), Equipment Maintenance Fund (Fund 672) and Building Maintenance
Fund (Fund 673). Funding for future years is subject to Council’s approval of the
citywide budgets for Fiscal Years 2027-2029.
sections.current_situation:
Public Works staff members work in operational capacities, frequently in potentially
hazardous situations. They require on-going job specific safety training to comply with
California Occupational Safety and Health Agency (Cal/OSHA) regulations and to
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@berkeleyca.gov Website: https://berkeleyca.gov/
Page 2 of 7
On-Call Safety Training Services Contracts CONSENT CALENDAR
Du-All Safety, LLC, Safe Business Solutions, Safety Center February 24, 2026
complete their work safely. This contract supports Cal/OSHA required training, as well
as training for administrative, technical and engineering staff.
The current contract for safety training services expires on March 31, 2026.
Authorization to enter new contracts with multiple vendors qualified to provide required
safety training services ensures continued Public Works’ compliance with Cal/OSHA
requirements and provides flexibility in scheduling to accommodate the various fixed
schedules of Public Works staff. The selected vendors will provide core training such
as:
 CPR/AED,
 First Aid,
 Hot Work,
 Confined Spaces,
 Fire Extinguisher, and
 Heat Illness Prevention.
Additionally, these vendors will develop a workplace Emergency Action Plan, all key to
preparing staff for quickly and safely responding to critical emergency situations such as
downed live wires, oxygen deficiency, excessive heat and sudden workplace fires.
Employee training is aligned with the City’s Strategic Plan Goal of creating a resilient,
safe, connected and prepared City. Further, safety training in topics such as Spill
Prevention, Storm Water Pollution Prevention, Asbestos Awareness, and Hazardous
Waste Management are also in alignment with the City’s Strategic Plan goal of
protecting the environment.
sections.background:
The City issued Request for Proposal Specification No. 25-11747-C for safety training
and consultation services on September 16, 2025, and received six responsive
proposals. A selection panel comprised of Department subject matter experts
evaluated the proposals based on the following criteria: Project Approach (30%),
Expertise and References (25%), Cost (25%), and Background (20%).
Du-All Safety, LLC., Safe Business Solutions, and Safety Center, Inc. were selected as
the most responsive, qualified, and cost-effective vendors to meet the needs of the
Department’s Safety Program.
This contract is not covered by California’s 2025 state legislation, AB 339, which
requires noticing of contract work to local public employee unions. Section 3504.1
of the California Government code explicitly states in Section 1(d) (1), that AB 339 does
not apply to the contracting of services related to, “…the planning, design,
administration, oversight, review, or delivery of public works, …or other infrastructure
projects subject to adopted uniform codes or standards.”
Page 3 of 7
On-Call Safety Training Services Contracts CONSENT CALENDAR
Du-All Safety, LLC, Safe Business Solutions, Safety Center February 24, 2026
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
All three selected vendors provide training in Hazardous Materials Handling, Spill
Response and Prevention, Stormwater Pollution Prevention and Asbestos
Management. Effective staff training protects staff and safeguards the community and
the environment from potentially hazardous and damaging situations.
sections.rationale:
The Department does not have capacity to provide all the safety training required for
staff and to maintain compliance with Cal/OSHA regulations. Program Managers do not
have specific expertise in the broad range of job-specific safety requirements necessary
to keep staff safe when working in the field.
The current contract is due to expire on March 31, 2026. The new contracts with more
targeted safety trainings services and a more robust scope of services will assist Public
Works in building out a more solid safety program that will ensure timely compliance
with critical regulatory requirements and help to keep staff and the community safe.
sections.alternatives:
Staff do not have capacity to operate the Department’s safety program without the
support of outside vendors working in conjunction with the Department’s Safety Officer.
As a result, the contracts with Du-All Safety, Inc., Safe Business Solutions, and Safety
Center, are critical to support the required safety training needs of Public Works.
CONTACT PERSON
Joy Brown, Public Works, Operations Manager, (510) 981-6629
Nicole Blake, Public Works, Safety Officer, (510) 981-6477

Page 4 of 7
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT: DU-ALL SAFETY, LLC FOR SAFETY TRAINING SERVICES TO
SUPPORT THE DEPARTMENT OF PUBLIC WORKS OPERATIONS DIVISIONS
WHEREAS, the City Public Works Department requires safety training for over 200
staffers in its Facilities, Streets and Utilities, Equipment Maintenance, and Zero Waste
Operations Divisions; and
WHEREAS, the City’s current safety training contract is set to expire on March 31, 2026;
and
WHEREAS, the City released RFP Specification No. 2511747-C on September 16, 2025;
and
WHEREAS, the City received six responsive proposals and a selection panel comprised
of subject matter experts from the Public Works Operations Divisions evaluated the
proposals; and
WHEREAS, Du-All Safety was selected as one of the most responsive and qualified
vendors; and
WHEREAS, ongoing Safety Training is imperative to maintaining Department compliance
with Cal OSHA; and
WHEREAS, funding is available in Fiscal Year 2026 adopted budget in Zero Waste Fund
(Fund 601), Sanitary Sewer Fund (Fund 611), Clean Storm Fund (Fund 616), Equipment
Maintenance Fund (Fund 672) and Building Maintenance Fund (Fund 673). Funding for
future Fiscal Years is subject to Council’s approval of the proposed cityward budget for
Fiscal Years 2027 – 2029.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager is authorized to execute a Contract and any subsequent Amendments with
Du-All Safety, LLC for Safety Training and Consultation Services to support the
Department of Public Works Operations Divisions for a period of three years from March
1, 2026 through February 28, 2029 for an amount Not-to-Exceed $70,000, and with the
option to extend the Contract for two additional one-year terms; with rates adjusted
annually in accordance with the Consumer Price Index.
Page 5 of 7
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRAC
